relations.subject: Savile's bustard
relations.evidence: Otididae are an Old-World and understudied family of birds mostly found in Afro-Tropical regions. It is found in Burkina Faso, Cameroon, Chad, Ivory Coast, Gambia, Mali, Mauritania, Niger, Nigeria, Senegal, and Sudan

relations.subject: Savile's bustard
relations.evidence: Found in the hotspots of the Bustard species which includes arid and semi-arid habitats. They live in bush and light woodland, near dried pools and clearings, and flat shrubs with Aristida grass and Acacia raddiana. This is similar to the rest of the family Otididae, which typically live in temperate and tropical lowland dry grassland habitats. Found in SW Mauritania and Senegal E through Mali, Burkina Faso, SW Niger, NE Nigeria, and Chad to C Sudan. The Savile's bustard used to be thought as restricted to the Sahel biome but has now been seen to have expanded southwards
relations.object_summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

relations.subject: Savile's bustard
relations.evidence: According to the IUCN Red List, the Savile's bustard is listed as least concern with their population trend being stable. Due to this species' large range, relatively high population densities, and no evidence of declines or substantial threats, it is not considered threatened. It appears to be fairly common in the Yankuri Game Reserve, Nigeria. The species was first recorded in N Cameroon in November 1995, in The Gambia in September 1996, and in N Benin in January 2005. Despite this species having a stable population trend, many bustard species populations are at risk and declining due to agricultural changes, overgrazing, hunting, trapping, habitat loss, and droughts.{{Cite journal|last1=Bailey|first1=T
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
